This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}
2026-04-08T03:50:58.7279989Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T03:48:10.476643108Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/list","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":null}}
2026-04-08T03:50:58.7336535Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T03:48:10.497501441Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get a specific label from a repository."},"description":"Get a specific label from a repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"name":{"description":"Label name.","type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner (username or organization name)","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo","name"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_label"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get issue details"},"description":"Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"issue_number":{"description":"The number of the issue","type":"number"},"method":{"description":"The read operation to perform on a single issue.\nOptions are:\n1. get - Get details of a specific issue.\n2. get_comments - Get issue comments.\n3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.\n4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.\n","enum":["get","get_comments","get_sub_issues","get_labels"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"The owner of the repository","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"The name of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"issue_read"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List available issue types"},"description":"List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"The organization owner of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["owner"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issue_types"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List issues"},"description":"List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"after":{"description":"Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-08T03:50:58.7436739Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T03:49:12.638508972Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57918,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: README Key Concepts missing convenience generators for ResponseEventStream\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57895 (`ecd2108`) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57895 added convenience generators to `ResponseEventStream` — `OutputItemMessage()` (string and `IAsyncEnumerable` overloads), `OutputItemFunctionCall()`, and `OutputItemReasoningItem()` — and restructured Samples 3, 4, and 6 with a convenience-first pattern. The changes promote convenience generators as the recommended approach for most `ResponseEventStream` use cases, relegating the full builder API to advanced scenarios only.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `README.md` **Key Concepts \\u0026gt; ResponseEventStream** section shows the full builder pattern (`stream.AddOutputItemMessage()`, `message.EmitAdded()`, etc.) as the primary example, with no mention of the new convenience generators (`stream.OutputItemMessage()`)\\n- The README description \\u0026#34;Use `ResponseEventStream` when you need multiple output types … fine-grained delta control … custom Response properties\\u0026#34; no longer reflects the convenience-first hierarchy introduced in this PR\\n- The README omits the streaming tokens overload `stream.OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable)`, which is now a first-class supported pattern documented in Sample 3\\n- The three-tier mental model (TextResponse → convenience generators → full builder) is fully documented in `samples/Sample3_FullControlResponseStream.md` but absent from the package README that most users read first\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts \\u0026gt; ResponseEventStream** section, replace the current block that reads:\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **`ResponseEventStream` — full control over every SSE event:**\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; Use `ResponseEventStream` when you need multiple output types (reasoning, function calls), fine-grained delta control, or custom Response properties.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_FullControl `\\n\\nwith content that introduces the three-tier hierarchy:\\n\\n1. `TextResponse` — simple text-only responses (already documented, no change)\\n2. `ResponseEventStream` with **convenience generators** — recommended for most non-text-only scenarios (NEW: `OutputItemMessage`, `OutputItemFunctionCall`, `OutputItemReasoningItem`)\\n3. `ResponseEventStream` with **full builder control** — for advanced scenarios (custom item properties, interleaved non-event work, multiple summary parts)\\n\\nThe convenience generator example to add is already captured in snippet `Responses_Sample3_GreetingHandlerConvenience`:\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler\\n{\\n    public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync(\\n        CreateResponse request,\\n        ResponseContext context,\\n        [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n    {\\n        await Task.CompletedTask;\\n        var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request);\\n        stream.Response.Temperature = 0.7;\\n        yield return stream.EmitCreated();\\n        yield return stream.EmitInProgress();\\n        foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($\\u0026#34;Hello! You said: \\\\\\u0026#34;{request.GetInputText()}\\\\\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;))\\n            yield return evt;\\n        yield return stream.EmitCompleted();\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAlso add or reference the streaming-tokens overload from snippet `Responses_Sample3_StreamingGreetingHandler`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Stream tokens as they arrive — each chunk becomes a delta event.\\nawait foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage(\\n    GenerateTokensAsync(request.GetInputText(), cancellationToken),\\n    cancellationToken))\\n{\\n    yield return evt;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nKeep the existing full-control builder snippet (`Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_FullControl`) below, clearly labeled as \\u0026#34;With full event control\\u0026#34; for advanced scenarios.\\n\\nUpdate the prose description to match Sample 3\\u0026#39;s explanation:\\n\\u0026gt; Use convenience generators when you need to emit function calls, reasoning items, or multiple outputs but don\\u0026#39;t need per-event control. Drop down to the builder API only when you need to set custom properties on output items before `EmitAdded()`, interleave non-event work between individual delta/done calls, or emit multiple summary parts within a single reasoning item.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippet for the convenience generator approach should be written in a test file so the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can inject it into the README automatically.\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleReadme.cs` (create or edit — check if a ReadMe sample test file already exists; if not, create one following the pattern in `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`)\\n\\nAdd a test method wrapping the convenience generator example:\\n\\n`````csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience\\n    // Use OutputItemMessage() for non-text-only responses without per-event control.\\n    // The convenience generator handles all inner events automatically.\\n    public class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler\\n    {\\n        public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync(\\n            CreateResponse request,\\n            ResponseContext context,\\n            [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n        {\\n            await Task.CompletedTask;\\n            var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request);\\n            yield return stream.EmitCreated();\\n            yield return stream.EmitInProgress();\\n            var input = request.GetInputText();\\n            foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($\\u0026#34;Hello! You said: \\\\\\u0026#34;{input}\\\\\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;))\\n                yield return evt;\\n            yield return stream.EmitCompleted();\\n        }\\n    }\\n    #endregion\\n    await Task.CompletedTask;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README placeholder** to insert in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`, under the `ResponseEventStream` key concept, after the prose introduction of convenience generators:\\n\\n`````\\n`````\\n```\\n`````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently has all required sections. Only the **Key Concepts \\u0026gt; ResponseEventStream** subsection needs to be updated:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started — Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started — Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started — Configure the server | ✅ Present |\\n| Key concepts — ResponseHandler | ✅ Present |\\n| Key concepts — TextResponse | ✅ Present |\\n| Key concepts — ResponseEventStream (convenience generators) | ❌ **Missing** |\\n| Key concepts — ResponseEventStream (full builder) | ✅ Present (but needs updated guidance text) |\\n| Key concepts — Streaming \\u0026amp; Background Modes | ✅ Present |\\n| Key concepts — Response Lifecycle | ✅ Present |\\n| Examples | ✅ Present |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm README was updated with the new snippet\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public API was added in this doc change, but run to confirm no regressions\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
